Title: Drought attenuates plant defence against bacterial pathogens by suppressing the expression of CBP60g/SARD1 during combined stress

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. CBP60g is strongly induced in Arabidopsis leaves by Pst DC3000 infection but this induction is drastically attenuated under combined moderate drought+Pst stress.
2. The reduction in CBP60g expression under combined stress is ABA-dependent, as CBP60g transcripts are higher in the ABA biosynthesis mutant aao3 than in WT under pathogen and combined stress.
3. Promoter activity measured using pCBP60g::GUS reporter lines is reduced by about 50% under combined stress compared with Pst alone, indicating transcriptional repression by drought/ABA.
4. cbp60g and cbp60g sard1 mutants show markedly higher bacterial loads and disease-associated chlorosis under both pathogen-alone and combined stress, with the double mutant being most susceptible.
5. Overexpression of CBP60g in the cbp60g background leads to ~40-fold higher CBP60g transcript levels and significantly reduces disease severity and bacterial CFU under combined stress relative to WT.
6. In npr3 and npr4 mutants, CBP60g expression under combined stress is higher than under pathogen-alone treatment and plants are more resistant, indicating that NPR3/4 mediate drought/ABA-induced repression of CBP60g.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Meta-analysis of public Arabidopsis drought and Pseudomonas syringae transcriptome datasets (29 independent drought microarray studies and six bacterial infection expression studies) to identify antagonistically regulated genes, revealing CBP60g and SARD1 as induced by bacteria but repressed under drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of SA-dependent immunity; CBP60g is required for robust resistance to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000, and its suppression under combined drought+pathogen stress leads to markedly higher disease severity and in planta bacterial growth. cbp60g and cbp60g sard1 mutants are highly susceptible, whereas CBP60g overexpression confers enhanced resistance under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: CBP60g functions as a transcriptional activator of SA biosynthesis and signalling genes; under combined drought+Pst stress, ABA signalling suppresses CBP60g transcription, at least partly via NPR3/4-mediated repression of CBP60g promoter activity, leading to reduced SA accumulation and attenuated defence gene induction.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: SA-mediated immune signalling including PTI, ETI, systemic acquired resistance and SA-independent defence branches, and ABA–SA crosstalk under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream transcriptional regulation of salicylic acid biosynthesis and signalling pathways.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ABA signalling --(negative transcriptional regulation)--> CBP60g under combined drought+Pst stress; NPR3/4 --(transcriptional repression)--> CBP60g under combined stress; CBP60g -->(activation)--> SA biosynthesis and signalling genes (e.g., SID2, EDS5, NPR1, PAD4, EDS1) and associated defence responses, as inferred from reduced SA and defence marker expression when CBP60g is suppressed.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calmodulin-binding Protein 60 family transcription factor; calmodulin-binding, plant-specific transcription factor implicated in SA-pathway regulation.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In rosette leaves, CBP60g transcripts are strongly induced by Pst DC3000 infection but this induction is drastically attenuated under combined drought+Pst stress; CBP60g shows a modest induction under drought or ABA alone and its induction under pathogen and combined stress is enhanced in the ABA-deficient aao3 mutant. pCBP60g::GUS reporter activity in leaves is reduced by about 50% under combined stress relative to Pst alone, indicating transcriptional repression by drought/ABA.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought (soil 60% field capacity decreasing to ~35% by 3 dpi), Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 infection, combined drought+Pst stress, and ABA/fluridone treatments that modulate CBP60g expression via ABA levels.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of plant immunity and hormone crosstalk under combined drought and bacterial pathogen stress, focusing on SA pathway control by CBP60g/SARD1.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (resistance to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato); combined abiotic–biotic stress response (moderate drought and bacterial infection).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: CBP60g-overexpressing plants show approximately 40-fold higher CBP60g transcript levels in unstressed leaves and significantly reduced disease severity and Pst CFU under combined stress compared with WT, whereas cbp60g and cbp60g sard1 mutants show dramatically higher chlorotic leaf area and bacterial CFU at 3 dpi under both pathogen-alone and combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: cbp60g and cbp60g sard1 mutants exhibit markedly increased disease-associated chlorosis and chlorotic leaf area under both Pst-alone and combined drought+Pst stress compared with Col-0; double mutants are more severely affected than the single cbp60g mutant.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 (WT), cbp60g-1 (AT5G26920; SALK_023199), sard1-2 (AT1G73805; SALK_052422), cbp60g-1 sard1-2 double mutant (CS72191), CBP60g-overexpressing line in cbp60g background driven by CaMV 35S promoter, and pCBP60g::GUS (2 kb CBP60g promoter fused to GUS) transgenic lines.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Growth of Col-0 and T-DNA mutants under controlled conditions; imposition of moderate drought by withholding irrigation to 60% field capacity followed by dip inoculation with Pst DC3000 to create combined stress; in planta bacterial CFU quantification at 0 and 3 dpi; disease scoring and chlorophyll/chlorotic area measurements; stomatal aperture measurements; exogenous ABA and fluridone sprays; LC-MS/MS-based quantification of SA and ABA; transcriptome meta-analysis of public drought and P. syringae datasets; RT-qPCR of CBP60g, SARD1, SA- and ABA-pathway marker genes; generation and analysis of pCBP60g::GUS reporter lines; generation and analysis of CBP60g overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis using cbp60g-1 loss-of-function mutant, cbp60g-1 sard1-2 double mutant, CBP60g-overexpressing transgenic lines, and pCBP60g::GUS reporter lines; disease scoring, chlorotic leaf area quantification, and in planta bacterial CFU assays under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress; RT-qPCR of CBP60g and defence marker genes; analysis in ABA-deficient aao3 mutant background.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene approach based on prior evidence that CBP60g regulates SA-mediated immunity; 1692 bp CBP60g CDS was amplified from Col-0 cDNA and cloned under the CaMV 35S promoter in pCAMBIA1302 to generate overexpression lines, and a 2 kb upstream promoter fragment was fused to GUS in pMDC164 to generate pCBP60g::GUS reporter plants.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: CBP60g is a master positive regulator of SA-mediated defence whose expression is specifically suppressed by moderate drought via ABA and NPR3/4 during bacterial infection. Loss of CBP60g (alone or together with SARD1) causes strongly enhanced disease and bacterial proliferation under pathogen and combined drought+Pst stress, whereas CBP60g overexpression restores resistance and lowers bacterial load under combined stress. Therefore, maintaining or enhancing CBP60g expression under drought (for example by drought-insensitive promoters or by weakening ABA/NPR3/4-mediated repression) could be used to breed or engineer crops with improved resistance to hemibiotrophic bacterial pathogens during moderate drought, while carefully balancing effects on drought responses.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SARD1, like CBP60g, is induced by Pst DC3000 infection but its induction is attenuated under combined moderate drought+Pst stress.
2. SARD1 expression under pathogen and combined stress is higher in the ABA biosynthesis mutant aao3 than in WT, indicating ABA-dependent repression under combined stress.
3. In the npr4 mutant, SARD1 expression is higher under combined stress than under pathogen-alone treatment, and npr4 plants are more resistant, implicating NPR4 in SARD1 repression.
4. cbp60g sard1 double mutants show more severe disease symptoms and higher diseased leaf area than cbp60g single mutants under both pathogen and combined stress, demonstrating additive roles of SARD1 and CBP60g in defence.
5. Suppression of SARD1, together with CBP60g, under combined stress contributes to reduced SA levels and compromised SA-mediated defences.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Meta-analysis of Arabidopsis drought and P. syringae transcriptomes showed that SARD1, like CBP60g, is induced by bacterial infection but downregulated by drought, highlighting it as a key node in antagonistic regulation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of SA-dependent immunity that acts additively with CBP60g; reduced SARD1 function, especially together with CBP60g loss, leads to strongly increased disease severity and bacterial proliferation under Pst and combined drought+Pst stress, indicating that SARD1 is indispensable for robust defence under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: SARD1 acts as a transcriptional activator of SA biosynthesis and signalling genes together with CBP60g. Under combined drought+Pst stress, ABA signalling suppresses SARD1 transcription, and NPR4 in particular contributes to repression of SARD1 expression, leading to reduced SA accumulation and weakened defence.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: SA-mediated defence signalling (PTI, ETI, SAR and SA-independent defence branches) and ABA–SA crosstalk during combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Upstream transcriptional regulation of salicylic acid biosynthesis and signalling pathways.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ABA signalling --(negative transcriptional regulation)--> SARD1 under combined drought+Pst stress; NPR4 --(transcriptional repression)--> SARD1 under combined stress (SARD1 expression higher in npr4 under combined stress); CBP60g/SARD1 -->(activation)--> SA biosynthesis/signalling genes and downstream defence responses, as inferred from altered SA and defence marker gene expression when this module is suppressed.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CBP60-related plant-specific transcription factor; homolog of calmodulin-binding protein CBP60g but functions independently of calmodulin binding.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In rosette leaves, SARD1 transcripts are induced by Pst infection and this induction is attenuated under combined drought+Pst stress, though the reduction is less steep than for CBP60g. SARD1 is not notably induced by drought alone but its expression under pathogen and combined stress is enhanced in the ABA-deficient aao3 mutant and in the npr4 mutant under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought (60% field capacity), Pst DC3000 infection, combined drought+Pst stress, and altered ABA levels in the aao3 mutant background.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Master transcriptional control of SA-mediated plant immunity and its modulation by drought/ABA signalling under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (resistance to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato); combined abiotic–biotic stress response (drought and bacterial infection).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: cbp60g sard1 double mutants show more severe disease-associated chlorosis and higher diseased leaf area than cbp60g single mutants under both pathogen-alone and combined stress, indicating additive roles of SARD1 and CBP60g in defence.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0, sard1-2 (AT1G73805; SALK_052422) single mutant, cbp60g-1 sard1-2 double mutant (CS72191), and npr3/npr4 mutants used to probe SARD1 regulation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Moderate drought and Pst DC3000 infection assays; in planta bacterial CFU measurements and disease scoring at 3 dpi; RT-qPCR quantification of SARD1 expression under control, drought, pathogen, combined stress, and in aao3, npr3, and npr4 mutants; hormone profiling and meta-transcriptome analysis to place SARD1 within SA–ABA crosstalk.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis using sard1-2 single mutant and cbp60g sard1 double mutant in Col-0 background; disease severity and in planta bacterial CFU assays under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress; RT-qPCR analysis of SARD1 expression under different stress and genetic (aao3, npr3, npr4) backgrounds.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: SARD1 was chosen as a candidate based on its known role as a CBP60g homolog and master regulator of SA-mediated immunity, and its antagonistic regulation by drought and bacterial stress identified in meta-transcriptome analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: SARD1 is a master transcription factor that acts partly redundantly with CBP60g to promote SA production and defence. Under combined drought+pathogen stress its induction is dampened, and cbp60g sard1 double mutants show greatly enhanced susceptibility compared with single mutants. Enhancing or stabilizing SARD1 (together with CBP60g) under drought could support SA accumulation and defence gene expression during bacterial attack, offering a breeding or engineering strategy to improve bacterial disease resistance in crops exposed to intermittent drought.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AAO3 encodes the aldehyde oxidase that catalyses the last step of ABA biosynthesis and is induced by drought stress.
2. Pharmacological ABA application enhances susceptibility to Pst, whereas the ABA biosynthesis inhibitor fluridone and the aao3 mutant reduce disease severity and bacterial load under both pathogen-only and combined drought+Pst stress.
3. Initial bacterial entry (0 dpi CFU) is similar in WT and aao3 plants, but at 3 dpi aao3 plants have significantly lower bacterial loads, indicating that AAO3-mediated ABA affects post-invasion defence.
4. Expression of CBP60g and SARD1 under pathogen and combined stress is higher in aao3 than in WT, showing that AAO3-dependent ABA negatively regulates these master immune regulators under combined stress.
5. AAO3 therefore links drought-induced ABA biosynthesis to suppression of SA-mediated defence via the CBP60g–SARD1 module.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: ABA levels were quantified by LC-MS/MS in WT plants under control, drought, pathogen and combined stress at multiple time points, linking AAO3-dependent ABA biosynthesis to dynamic hormone changes and defence outcomes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key ABA biosynthesis enzyme that promotes susceptibility to Pst; aao3 loss-of-function plants are more resistant and show lower in planta bacterial loads than WT under both pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress, indicating that AAO3-mediated ABA production contributes to suppression of defence in these conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: AAO3 catalyses the final oxidation step in ABA biosynthesis, increasing ABA levels that in turn repress CBP60g/SARD1 expression and SA-mediated defences under combined drought+Pst stress, thereby promoting susceptibility.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Abscisic acid biosynthesis and signalling pathway, involved in drought responses and antagonistic cross-talk with SA-mediated defence under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Abscisic acid biosynthesis pathway (final oxidation of abscisic aldehyde to ABA).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: AAO3 -->(ABA biosynthesis)--> elevated ABA levels; ABA signalling --(negative regulation)--> CBP60g and SARD1 expression under combined drought+Pst stress; AAO3 loss-of-function -->(reduced ABA)--> higher CBP60g/SARD1 expression and enhanced resistance to Pst in both pathogen-only and combined stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: AAO3 is induced under drought stress but its transcript level under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress is similar to control; nonetheless, ABA levels are elevated under drought and pathogen conditions, and defence phenotypes indicate AAO3-dependent ABA contributes to susceptibility. In the aao3 mutant CBP60g and SARD1 induction under pathogen and combined stress is higher than in WT, consistent with reduced ABA-mediated repression.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought (60% field capacity), Pst DC3000 infection, combined drought+Pst stress, and exogenous ABA and fluridone treatments that modulate ABA levels.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of ABA biosynthesis in modulating plant immunity and SA–ABA antagonism under combined drought and bacterial pathogen stress.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought response via ABA); biotic stress (susceptibility/resistance to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato) and combined abiotic–biotic stress response.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under combined drought+Pst stress, aao3 mutants show significantly reduced in planta bacterial CFU and disease severity at 3 dpi compared to WT, while initial bacterial loads at 0 dpi are similar, indicating a post-invasion effect of AAO3-dependent ABA.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 and aao3-4 mutant (AT2G27150; SALK_072361) defective in AAO3, in the Col-0 background.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Moderate drought and Pst DC3000 infection assays in WT and aao3 plants; in planta bacterial CFU quantification at 0 and 3 dpi; visual disease assessment; RT-qPCR analysis of AAO3, ABA3 and NCED3 expression under control, drought, pathogen and combined stress; LC-MS/MS-based ABA quantification time course; RT-qPCR of CBP60g and SARD1 in aao3 versus WT under pathogen and combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of aao3-4 ABA biosynthesis mutant (AT2G27150; SALK_072361) in Col-0 background; comparison of disease severity and in planta bacterial CFU at 0 and 3 dpi under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress between WT and aao3; RT-qPCR of AAO3 and other ABA biosynthesis genes; hormone quantification and genetic interaction with CBP60g/SARD1 expression.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: AAO3 was selected based on its known role as the final enzyme in ABA biosynthesis and its inclusion among key ABA pathway genes; functional analysis performed using the aao3-4 T-DNA insertion mutant.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AAO3 catalyses the last step of ABA biosynthesis and promotes susceptibility to Pst both under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress. The aao3 mutant, with reduced ABA production, shows significantly lower bacterial loads and disease severity. Modulating AAO3 activity or expression could therefore be used to reduce drought-induced suppression of defence and improve bacterial disease resistance during moderate drought, though potential trade-offs with drought tolerance must be considered.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. NPR3 is a transcriptional repressor of CBP60g/SARD1 identified as an upstream regulator of these master immune genes.
2. CBP60g expression under combined drought+Pst stress is dramatically higher in npr3 mutants than under pathogen-alone treatment, indicating that NPR3 mediates repression of CBP60g under combined stress.
3. npr3 mutants show markedly reduced disease severity and in planta bacterial loads at 3 dpi under both pathogen-only and combined stress compared with WT, while initial bacterial entry is unaffected.
4. NPR3 transcript levels do not differ between pathogen and combined stress, nor between WT and aao3, suggesting that ABA does not regulate NPR3 at the transcriptional level in these conditions.
5. NPR3 thus functions as a key negative regulator that links SA signalling to suppression of the CBP60g–SARD1 module under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of SA-dependent immunity under combined stress; npr3 mutant plants display enhanced resistance and dramatically lower Pst CFU at 3 dpi compared with WT under both pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress, associated with increased CBP60g expression under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: NPR3 acts as an SA-responsive transcriptional corepressor; under combined drought+Pst stress it mediates repression of CBP60g (and to a lesser extent SARD1), thereby suppressing SA biosynthesis and defence signalling. NPR3 is not transcriptionally controlled by ABA under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Salicylic acid signalling pathway and ABA–SA crosstalk in the context of combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: NPR3 --(transcriptional repression)--> CBP60g (strong effect) and contributes to repression of SARD1 under combined drought+Pst stress; SA -->(binding to NPR3) modulates NPR3 repressor activity (as established in SA signalling), while ABA does not alter NPR3 transcript abundance under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPR1-like SA receptor/corepressor protein involved in SA-responsive transcriptional regulation.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: NPR3 transcript levels do not differ significantly between pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst treatments or between WT and aao3 mutants, indicating that its role in combined stress is mediated at the level of its regulatory activity rather than transcriptional induction.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought (60% field capacity), Pst DC3000 infection, combined drought+Pst stress, and altered ABA levels via the aao3 mutant used to test whether ABA regulates NPR3 transcription.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Negative regulation of plant immunity and SA signalling under combined drought and bacterial pathogen stress via NPR1-like corepressors.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (resistance/susceptibility to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato); combined abiotic–biotic stress response (drought and bacterial infection).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under pathogen and combined drought+Pst stress, npr3 mutants show markedly reduced in planta bacterial CFU at 3 dpi compared with WT, while initial bacterial loads at 0 dpi are similar, indicating a strong effect on post-invasion defence.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 and npr3-2 mutant (AT5G45110; SALK_043055) in the Col-0 background.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Moderate drought and Pst DC3000 infection assays in WT and npr3 plants; RT-qPCR quantification of CBP60g and SARD1 expression at 24 hpi under control, drought, pathogen and combined stress; disease imaging and in planta bacterial CFU quantification at 0 and 3 dpi.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of npr3-2 T-DNA insertion mutant (AT5G45110; SALK_043055) in Col-0 background; RT-qPCR analysis of CBP60g and SARD1 expression under pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst stress; phenotypic assessment and in planta bacterial CFU measurements comparing Col-0 and npr3 under these treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: NPR3 was selected based on its known role as an SA-responsive transcriptional corepressor of defence genes and of CBP60g/SARD1; its function under combined stress was probed using an existing T-DNA knockout line.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: NPR3 functions as a transcriptional corepressor that suppresses CBP60g (and thereby SA-mediated immunity) under combined drought+Pst stress. npr3 mutants show elevated CBP60g expression and strong resistance with reduced bacterial loads under pathogen and combined stress. Thus, reducing NPR3 activity could be a strategy to maintain SA-dependent defence under drought and improve resistance to bacterial pathogens, while monitoring possible impacts on SA signalling homeostasis.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. NPR4 is a transcriptional repressor of CBP60g and SARD1 implicated as an upstream regulator in the suppression of these immune regulators under combined drought+Pst stress.
2. CBP60g and SARD1 expression under combined stress is higher in npr4 mutants than under pathogen-alone treatment, indicating that NPR4 mediates repression of both genes in the combined stress context.
3. npr4 mutants show markedly reduced disease severity and in planta bacterial loads at 3 dpi under both pathogen-only and combined stress relative to WT, while initial bacterial entry is unchanged.
4. NPR4 transcript levels are similar between pathogen-alone and combined stress and between WT and aao3, suggesting that its regulation by ABA is not at the mRNA level.
5. NPR4, together with NPR3, constitutes a key repressor module that attenuates the CBP60g–SARD1 branch of SA-mediated immunity under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of SA-dependent immunity under combined stress; npr4 mutant plants exhibit higher CBP60g and SARD1 expression under combined stress and show enhanced resistance and markedly reduced Pst bacterial loads compared with WT under both pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst treatments.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: NPR4 acts as an SA-responsive transcriptional corepressor; under combined drought+Pst stress it represses CBP60g and SARD1, thereby weakening SA biosynthesis and signalling. NPR4 transcription is not regulated by ABA under combined stress, suggesting ABA may modulate its activity at a post-transcriptional level.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Salicylic acid signalling network and ABA–SA crosstalk controlling plant immunity under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: NPR4 --(transcriptional repression)--> CBP60g and SARD1 under combined drought+Pst stress; SA -->(binding to NPR4) modulates its corepressor activity in SA signalling, whereas ABA does not alter NPR4 transcript abundance under combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPR1-like SA receptor/corepressor protein involved in negative regulation of SA-responsive transcription.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: NPR4 transcript levels are not significantly different between pathogen-alone and combined drought+Pst treatments or between WT and aao3 mutants, indicating that changes in its regulatory activity, rather than its mRNA level, underlie its role in combined stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought (60% field capacity), Pst DC3000 infection, combined drought+Pst stress, and altered ABA levels via the aao3 mutant used to test for ABA-dependent transcriptional control of NPR4.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of NPR1-like corepressors in modulating SA-mediated defence and CBP60g/SARD1 expression under combined drought and bacterial stress.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (resistance/susceptibility to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato); combined abiotic–biotic stress response (drought and bacterial infection).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: npr4 mutants display substantially reduced in planta Pst CFU at 3 dpi under both pathogen-only and combined drought+Pst stress compared with WT, while 0 dpi bacterial loads are similar, demonstrating a strong enhancement of post-invasion defence.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 and npr4-2 mutant (AT4G19660; SALK_098460) in the Col-0 background.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Moderate drought and Pst DC3000 infection assays in WT and npr4 plants; RT-qPCR measurement of CBP60g and SARD1 expression under control, drought, pathogen and combined stress; visual assessment of disease symptoms and in planta bacterial CFU quantification at 0 and 3 dpi.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of npr4-2 T-DNA insertion mutant (AT4G19660; SALK_098460) in Col-0 background; RT-qPCR analysis of CBP60g and SARD1 expression under pathogen and combined drought+Pst stress; assessment of disease symptoms and in planta bacterial CFU at 0 and 3 dpi in Col-0 versus npr4 plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: NPR4 was chosen based on its established role as an SA-responsive transcriptional corepressor and repressor of CBP60g/SARD1; its role under combined stress was tested using an existing T-DNA knockout line.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: NPR4, together with NPR3, represses CBP60g and SARD1 under combined drought+Pst stress. npr4 mutants show increased expression of both CBP60g and SARD1 and strong resistance with lower bacterial loads under pathogen and combined stress. Targeting NPR4 (alone or with NPR3) could help maintain SA-mediated defences and reduce drought-induced disease susceptibility in crops, while carefully managing potential impacts on SA signalling balance.
